Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion notes BRG1 is not suggested to be a transcription factor and likely acts indirectly on GA biosynthesis gene expression.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession Numbers section lists SINA1 as Solyc01g006190.

Source_Species.reason: SINA1 is a tomato gene in this study.

Applied_Species.reason: SINA1 transgenic analyses were in tomato.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Transgenics were generated in Ailsa Craig.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Figure 7A shows reduced GA1 in SINA1-OX plants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper describes GA as growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation.

Regulator_Type.reason: SINA1 is explicitly a RING-dependent ubiquitin ligase / ubiquitin ligase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper identifies SINA1 as belonging to Seven in absentia family with RING-dependent ubiquitin ligase activity.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Discussion states SINA1 is localized in nucleus and cytoplasm, citing prior work within study context.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results link SINA1-mediated BSD1 degradation to regulation of GA biosynthesis and vegetative growth.

Regulation_Mode.reason: SINA1 promotes ubiquitin-mediated proteasomal degradation of BSD1.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: BRG1 is downregulated in SINA1-OX; GA biosynthesis is suppressed indirectly via BSD1 degradation according to Results/Discussion.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: BSD1 is directly shown as SINA1 substrate.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Discussion states SINA proteins can homo-/heterodimerize and may affect BSD1 regulation, though unresolved.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Paper directly shows SINA1 interacts with BSD1; discussion suggests dimerization with other SINAs including SINA2/3.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: SINA1-OX plants are dwarf with reduced GA1, indicating negative regulation of GA production and vegetative growth.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Data support SINA1 as upstream negative regulator reducing GA biosynthesis by lowering BSD1 abundance.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present in Results/Methods.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by sections 2.3-2.5 and 2.8.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential use of SINA1 loss/downregulation is an inference; the paper does not explicitly propose this application.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly mentions possible pleiotropy/additional substrates and interactions with other SINAs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: GA1 was measured in internode stems.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: MG132/MG115 and exogenous GA3 are all used in mechanistic/phenotypic assays.

Omics_Data.reason: Paper reports RNA-seq analysis on SINA1-OX vs AC, with no accession provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper explicitly states SINA1/2/3 interact with BSD1 in plant cells, but only SINA1 ubiquitinates BSD1 in vitro.
